if (palindrome.size() == 1) return "";

for (int i = 0; i < palindrome.size() / 2; ++i) {

solution:
```class Solution {
public:
    string breakPalindrome(string palindrome) {
        int n = palindrome.size();
        if (n == 1) return "";
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; ++i) {
            if (palindrome[i] != 'a') {
                palindrome[i] = 'a';
                return palindrome;
            }
        }
        
        palindrome.back() = 'b';
        return palindrome;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the first half of the string to find the earliest position to change to 'a', ensuring the smallest lex order. If all are 'a's, it changes the last character to 'b'. This approach efficiently breaks the palindrome with minimal changes.
